   315→    pub protocol_version: String,
   316→    pub id: u64,
   317→    pub status: String,
   318→
   319→    // Handshake fields
   320→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   321→    pub frontend_version: Option<String>,
   322→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   323→    pub language: Option<String>,
   324→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   325→    pub capabilities: Option<Vec<String>>,
   326→
   327→    // Setup fields
   328→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   329→    pub setup_context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   330→
   331→    // Generate fields
   332→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   333→    pub value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   334→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   335→    pub generator_id: Option<String>,
   336→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   337→    pub recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   338→
   339→    // Instrument fields
   340→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   341→    pub instrumented: Option<bool>,
   342→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   343→    pub output_file: Option<String>,
   344→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   345→    pub instrumentable_line_count: Option<u32>,
   346→
   347→    // Analyze fields
   348→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   349→    pub functions: Option<Vec<FunctionAnalysis>>,
   350→
   351→    // Execute result fields — flattened to match the core's wire format.
   352→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   353→    pub return_value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   354→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   355→    pub thrown_error: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   356→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   357→    pub branch_path: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   358→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   359→    pub lines_executed: Option<Vec<u32>>,
   360→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   361→    pub calls_to_external: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   362→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   363→    pub path_constraints: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   364→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   365→    pub side_effects: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   366→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   367→    pub performance: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   368→
   369→    // Error fields
   370→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   371→    pub code: Option<String>,
   372→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   373→    pub message: Option<String>,
   374→}

   376→impl Response {
   377→    /// Create a base response with protocol version and request ID.
   378→    pub fn base(id: u64) -> Self {
   379→        Self {
   380→            protocol_version: PROTOCOL_VERSION.to_string(),
   381→            id,
   382→            status: String::new(),
   383→            frontend_version: None,
   384→            language: None,
   385→            capabilities: None,
   386→            setup_context: None,
   387→            value: None,
   388→            generator_id: None,
   389→            recipe: None,
   390→            instrumented: None,
   391→            output_file: None,
   392→            instrumentable_line_count: None,
   393→            functions: None,
   394→            return_value: None,
   395→            thrown_error: None,
   396→            branch_path: None,
   397→            lines_executed: None,
   398→            calls_to_external: None,
   399→            path_constraints: None,
   400→            side_effects: None,
   401→            performance: None,
   402→            code: None,
   403→            message: None,
   404→        }
   405→    }
   406→}

   412→    fn round_trip<T: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + PartialEq + std::fmt::Debug>(
   413→        value: &T,
   414→    ) {
   415→        let json = serde_json::to_string(value).expect("serialize");
   416→        let deserialized: T = serde_json::from_str(&json).expect("deserialize");
   417→        assert_eq!(*value, deserialized, "round-trip failed for json: {json}");
   418→    }
   419→
   420→    #[test]
   421→    fn typeinfo_opaque_round_trips() {
   422→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Opaque {
   423→            label: "net.Socket".to_string(),
   424→        });
   425→    }
   426→

   429→        let ti = TypeInfo::Opaque {

   432→        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&ti).expect("serialize");
   433→        assert_eq!(json["kind"], "opaque");
   434→        assert_eq!(json["label"], "fs.FileHandle");
   435→    }

   437→    #[test]

   500→    #[test]
   501→    fn opaque_in_function_analysis_json_deserializes() {
   502→        // Verify TypeInfo::Opaque works when embedded in a FunctionAnalysis-shaped JSON,
   503→        // parsed as a generic Value and then extracting the type field.
   504→        let json = r#"{"kind": "opaque", "label": "stream.Readable"}"#;
   505→        let param_type: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize param type");
   506→        assert_eq!(
   507→            param_type,
   508→            TypeInfo::Opaque {
   509→                label: "stream.Readable".to_string(),
   510→            }
   511→        );
   512→
   513→        // Nested inside an object field (simulating a return_type in analysis results)
   514→        let nested_json = r#"{"kind": "object", "fields": [["conn", {"kind": "opaque", "label": "pg.Client"}], ["ready", {"kind": "bool"}]]}"#;
   515→        let nested: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(nested_json).expect("deserialize nested");
   516→        if let TypeInfo::Object { fields } = &nested {
   517→            assert_eq!(fields.len(), 2);
   518→            assert_eq!(
   519→                fields[0].1,
   520→                TypeInfo::Opaque {
   521→                    label: "pg.Client".to_string(),
   522→                }
   523→            );
   524→        } else {
   525→            panic!("expected Object, got {:?}", nested);
   526→        }
   527→    }

   529→    // -- Request deserialization tests for new commands --

   531→    #[test]
   532→    fn setup_request_deserializes() {
   533→        let json = r#"{"protocol_version":"0.1.0","id":20,"command":"setup","file":"./setup.rs","scope":"processOrder","level":"function"}"#;
   534→        let req: Request = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize setup");
   535→        assert_eq!(req.id, 20);

/home/ketan/project/shatter/.claude/worktrees/str-fpgb.2/scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Validate that protocol/registry.yaml matches the source-of-truth code.
     3→
     4→Checks commands, response statuses, and error codes in:
     5→  - shatter-core/src/protocol.rs  (Rust core)
     6→  - shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts    (TypeScript frontend)
     7→  - shatter-go/protocol/          (Go frontend)
     8→  - shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs  (Rust frontend)
     9→
    10→Exit 0 if everything matches, 1 on any mismatch.
    11→"""
    12→
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import os
    16→import re
    17→import sys
    18→from pathlib import Path
    19→
    20→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→# Resolve paths relative to the repo root (parent of this script's directory)
    22→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    24→REPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent
    25→
    26→REGISTRY_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "protocol" / "registry.yaml"
    27→CORE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-core" / "src" / "protocol.rs"
    28→TS_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-ts" / "src" / "protocol.ts"
    29→GO_CONSTANTS = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "constants.go"
    30→GO_HANDLER = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "handler.go"
    31→RUST_FE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-rust" / "src" / "protocol.rs"
    32→
    33→
    34→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→# Minimal YAML parser — avoids PyYAML dependency
    36→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→
    38→def parse_registry(path: Path) -> dict:
    39→    """Extract commands, statuses, and error_codes from registry.yaml.
    40→
    41→    Returns dict with keys 'commands', 'statuses', 'error_codes' each
    42→    mapping to a set of strings.
    43→    """
    44→    text = path.read_text()
    45→
    46→    def extract_top_level_keys(section: str) -> set[str]:
    47→        """Extract keys under a top-level YAML mapping section."""
    48→        pattern = rf"^{re.escape(section)}:\s*$"
    49→        match = re.search(pattern, text, re.MULTILINE)
    50→        if not match:
    51→            return set()
    52→        start = match.end()
    53→        keys: set[str] = set()
    54→        for line in text[start:].splitlines():
    55→            if not line or line.startswith("#"):
    56→                continue
    57→            # End of section: non-indented, non-blank line
    58→            if line[0] not in (" ", "\t"):
    59→                break
    60→            # Key line: exactly 2-space indent, word followed by colon
    61→            m = re.match(r"^  ([a-z_]+):", line)
    62→            if m:
    63→                keys.add(m.group(1))
    64→        return keys
    65→
    66→    return {
    67→        "commands": extract_top_level_keys("commands"),
    68→        "statuses": extract_top_level_keys("statuses"),
    69→        "error_codes": extract_top_level_keys("error_codes"),
    70→    }
    71→
    72→
    73→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→# Source extractors
    75→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→
    77→def extract_rust_core(path: Path) -> dict:
    78→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-core protocol.rs."""
    79→    text = path.read_text()
    80→
    81→    # Commands: enum Command variants (PascalCase → snake_case)
    82→    commands: set[str] = set()
    83→    in_command = False
    84→    for line in text.splitlines():
    85→        if re.match(r"^pub enum Command\b", line):
    86→            in_command = True
    87→            continue
    88→        if in_command:
    89→            if line.startswith("}"):
    90→                break
    91→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
    92→            if m:
    93→                commands.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
    94→
    95→    # ResponseResult: enum variants → statuses
    96→    statuses: set[str] = set()
    97→    in_resp = False
    98→    for line in text.splitlines():
    99→        if re.match(r"^pub enum ResponseResult\b", line):
   100→            in_resp = True
   101→            continue
   102→        if in_resp:
   103→            if line.startswith("}"):
   104→                break
   105→            m = re.match(r"\s+#", line)
   106→            if m:
   107→                continue
   108→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
   109→            if m:
   110→                statuses.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
   111→
   112→    # ErrorCode enum
   113→    error_codes: set[str] = set()
   114→    in_err = False
   115→    for line in text.splitlines():
   116→        if re.match(r"^pub enum ErrorCode\b", line):
   117→            in_err = True
   118→            continue
   119→        if in_err:
   120→            if line.startswith("}"):
   121→                break
   122→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)", line)
   123→            if m and not m.group(1).startswith("//") and not m.group(1).startswith("#"):
   124→                error_codes.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
   125→
   126→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   127→
   128→
   129→def extract_ts(path: Path) -> dict:
   130→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-ts protocol.ts."""
   131→    text = path.read_text()
   132→
   133→    commands = extract_ts_union(text, "Command")
   134→    statuses = extract_ts_union(text, "ResponseStatus")
   135→    error_codes = extract_ts_union(text, "ErrorCode")
   136→
   137→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   138→
   139→
   140→def extract_ts_union(text: str, type_name: str) -> set[str]:
   141→    """Extract string literal members from a TypeScript union type."""
   142→    pattern = rf"type\s+{type_name}\s*=\s*([^;]+);"
   143→    m = re.search(pattern, text, re.DOTALL)
   144→    if not m:
   145→        return set()
   146→    return set(re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', m.group(1)))
   147→
   148→
   149→def extract_go(constants_path: Path, handler_path: Path) -> dict:
   150→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from Go frontend."""
   151→    const_text = constants_path.read_text()
   152→    handler_text = handler_path.read_text()
   153→
   154→    # Error codes from constants.go
   155→    error_codes: set[str] = set()
   156→    for m in re.finditer(r'Err\w+\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"', const_text):
   157→        error_codes.add(m.group(1))
   158→    # Inline error codes in handler.go
   159→    for m in re.finditer(r'Code:\s*"([^"]+)"', handler_text):
   160→        error_codes.add(m.group(1))
   161→
   162→    # Commands from CommandCapabilities in constants.go
   163→    commands: set[str] = set()
   164→    cap_match = re.search(r"CommandCapabilities\s*=.*?\{([^}]+)\}", const_text, re.DOTALL)
   165→    if cap_match:
   166→        commands = set(re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', cap_match.group(1)))
   167→    # Also add handshake and shutdown (always handled, not in capabilities)
   168→    commands.add("handshake")
   169→    commands.add("shutdown")
   170→
   171→    # Statuses from handler.go: resp.Status = "..."
   172→    statuses: set[str] = set()
   173→    for m in re.finditer(r'\.Status\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"', handler_text):
   174→        statuses.add(m.group(1))
   175→
   176→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   177→
   178→
   179→def extract_rust_fe(path: Path) -> dict:
   180→    """Extract commands and error codes from shatter-rust protocol.rs."""
   181→    text = path.read_text()
   182→
   183→    # Commands: look for command string matches in dispatch
   184→    commands: set[str] = set()
   185→    for m in re.finditer(r'"(handshake|analyze|instrument|execute|setup|teardown|generate|shutdown)"', text):
   186→        commands.add(m.group(1))
   187→
   188→    # Error codes
   189→    error_codes: set[str] = set()
   190→    for m in re.finditer(r'code.*?"([a-z_]+)"', text):
   191→        val = m.group(1)
   192→        # Filter to actual error codes (not random strings)
   193→        if val in {
   194→            "file_not_found", "function_not_found", "parse_error",
   195→            "instrumentation_failed", "execution_timeout", "execution_crash",
   196→            "version_mismatch", "invalid_request", "compilation_error",
   197→            "internal_error", "not_supported",
   198→        }:
   199→            error_codes.add(val)
   200→
   201→    # Statuses
   202→    statuses: set[str] = set()
   203→    for m in re.finditer(r'status.*?"([a-z_]+)"', text):
   204→        statuses.add(m.group(1))
   205→
   206→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   207→
   208→
   209→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   210→# Helpers
   211→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   212→
   213→def pascal_to_snake(name: str) -> str:
   214→    """Convert PascalCase to snake_case."""
   215→    s1 = re.sub(r"(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)", r"\1_\2", name)
   216→    return re.sub(r"([a-z0-9])([A-Z])", r"\1_\2", s1).lower()
   217→
   218→
   219→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   220→# Validation
   221→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   222→
   223→def validate(registry: dict, source_name: str, source: dict) -> list[str]:
   224→    """Compare registry entries against a source; return list of issues."""
   225→    issues: list[str] = []
   226→    for category in ("commands", "statuses", "error_codes"):
   227→        reg_set = registry[category]
   228→        src_set = source.get(category, set())
   229→        if not src_set:
   230→            continue
   231→

I have all the information needed. Here is the implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Error Code Contract Parity (str-fpgb.2)

### Problem Summary

The canonical error code list in `protocol/registry.yaml` defines 11 codes, but every downstream consumer has gaps:

| Location | Count | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| `registry.yaml` (canonical) | 11 | -- |
| `response.schema.json` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `PROTOCOL.md` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| Rust core `ErrorCode` enum | 10 | `not_supported` |
| Rust core `all_error_codes_round_trip` test | 9 | `compilation_error` (enum exists but test omitted) |
| Rust core `arb_error_code()` | 10 | `not_supported` |
| TS `ErrorCode` union type | 10 | `not_supported` |
| TS `arbErrorCode` generator | 10 | `not_supported` |
| Go `constants.go` | 9 | `function_not_found`, `instrumentation_failed` |
| Go `property_test.go` error generator | 4 | 7 codes missing from `SampledFrom` |
| Rust frontend `handler.rs` | uses `"non_executable"` | NOT a standard code |

### Decision: `non_executable` mapping

The `non_executable` error code in `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` (line 383) fires when a function has trait-object parameters that cannot be deserialized. This maps cleanly to the existing `not_supported` code -- "Command not supported by this frontend" covers the case where a function cannot be executed due to language-level constraints. No new code needs to be added to the registry.

### Order of Operations

The guiding principle: fix the canonical source and derived artifacts first (top-down), then fix each consumer, then add parity tests that prevent future drift. Each step is independently testable.

#### Phase 1: Schema and Documentation (no code changes, prevents future drift)

**Step 1.1: Update `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json`**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/protocol/schemas/response.schema.json`
- Change: Add `"compilation_error"` and `"not_supported"` to the `error_response.$defs.error_response.properties.code.enum` array (line 184-189).
- The array should match `registry.yaml` exactly: `["file_not_found", "function_not_found", "parse_error", "instrumentation_failed", "execution_timeout", "execution_crash", "version_mismatch", "invalid_request", "compilation_error", "internal_error", "not_supported"]`

**Step 1.2: Update `PROTOCOL.md`**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/PROTOCOL.md`
- Change: Add two rows to the Error Codes table (around line 322-332):
  - `compilation_error` | Compilation or transpilation failed.
  - `not_supported` | Command not supported by this frontend.

#### Phase 2: Rust Core (shatter-core)

**Step 2.1: Add `NotSupported` variant to `ErrorCode` enum**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`
- Change: Add `NotSupported` variant after `InternalError` (around line 458), with doc comment `/// Command or operation not supported by this frontend.`

**Step 2.2: Fix `all_error_codes_round_trip` test**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`
- Change: Add `ErrorCode::CompilationError` and `ErrorCode::NotSupported` to the `codes` array in the `all_error_codes_round_trip` test (line 871). This test was already missing `CompilationError` despite the variant existing.

**Step 2.3: Update `arb_error_code()` shared generator**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-core/src/test_arbitraries.rs`
- Change: Add `Just(ErrorCode::NotSupported)` to `arb_error_code()` (around line 178).

**Step 2.4: Add exhaustiveness parity test**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-core/src/protocol.rs` (in the `#[cfg(test)]` module)
- Add a new test `error_code_count_matches_registry` that asserts the number of variants in the `arb_error_code` generator equals the expected count (11). This catches future additions to the enum that aren't reflected in the generator.
- Better approach: add a compile-time exhaustiveness check using a `match` on `ErrorCode` with all variants listed, so that adding a variant without updating the test causes a compiler error:
  ```rust
  #[test]
  fn error_code_enum_is_exhaustive() {
      // This match ensures that if a new ErrorCode variant is added,
      // this test must be updated (and by extension, the round-trip test
      // and arb_error_code generator).
      fn check_exhaustive(code: ErrorCode) -> &'static str {
          match code {
              ErrorCode::FileNotFound => "file_not_found",
              ErrorCode::FunctionNotFound => "function_not_found",
              ErrorCode::ParseError => "parse_error",
              ErrorCode::InstrumentationFailed => "instrumentation_failed",
              ErrorCode::ExecutionTimeout => "execution_timeout",
              ErrorCode::ExecutionCrash => "execution_crash",
              ErrorCode::VersionMismatch => "version_mismatch",
              ErrorCode::InvalidRequest => "invalid_request",
              ErrorCode::CompilationError => "compilation_error",
              ErrorCode::InternalError => "internal_error",
              ErrorCode::NotSupported => "not_supported",
          }
      }
      // Verify the serialized form matches the expected string for each variant.
      let all_codes = [
          ErrorCode::FileNotFound, ErrorCode::FunctionNotFound, ErrorCode::ParseError,
          ErrorCode::InstrumentationFailed, ErrorCode::ExecutionTimeout, ErrorCode::ExecutionCrash,
          ErrorCode::VersionMismatch, ErrorCode::InvalidRequest, ErrorCode::CompilationError,
          ErrorCode::InternalError, ErrorCode::NotSupported,
      ];
      for code in all_codes {
          let expected = check_exhaustive(code.clone());
          let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&code).unwrap();
          assert_eq!(serialized, format!("\"{}\"", expected));
      }
  }
  ```

#### Phase 3: TypeScript Frontend (shatter-ts)

**Step 3.1: Add `not_supported` to `ErrorCode` type**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts`
- Change: Add `| "not_supported"` to the `ErrorCode` union type (after line 145).

**Step 3.2: Update `arbErrorCode` generator**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`
- Change: Add `"not_supported"` to the `fc.constantFrom(...)` call (line 83-87).

**Step 3.3: Add parity test**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`
- Add a test that verifies the `arbErrorCode` generator covers exactly the same codes as the `ErrorCode` type. Since TS union types are erased at runtime, the practical approach is to maintain a `const ALL_ERROR_CODES` array in `protocol.ts` and have both the type and the test reference it:
  - In `protocol.ts`: add `export const ALL_ERROR_CODES = ["file_not_found", "function_not_found", ...] as const;` and derive `ErrorCode` from it: `export type ErrorCode = (typeof ALL_ERROR_CODES)[number];`
  - In `property.test.ts`: change `arbErrorCode` to `fc.constantFrom(...ALL_ERROR_CODES)` and add a test asserting `ALL_ERROR_CODES.length === 11`.

#### Phase 4: Go Frontend (shatter-go)

**Step 4.1: Add missing constants**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/constants.go`
- Change: Add `ErrFunctionNotFound = "function_not_found"` and `ErrInstrumentationFailed = "instrumentation_failed"` to the `const` block. These are already used as string literals in `handler.go` (lines 187, 283).

**Step 4.2: Replace string literals with constants in handler.go**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/handler.go`
- Change: Replace `"function_not_found"` (lines 187, 281) with `ErrFunctionNotFound`, and `"instrumentation_failed"` (line 283) with `ErrInstrumentationFailed`. Also audit all other `resp.Code = "..."` assignments and replace with constants.

**Step 4.3: Replace string literals in handler_test.go**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/handler_test.go`
- Change: Replace `"function_not_found"` (lines 258, 439) with `ErrFunctionNotFound`.

**Step 4.4: Add `AllErrorCodes` slice and update property test generator**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/constants.go`
- Add: `var AllErrorCodes = []string{ErrFileNotFound, ErrFunctionNotFound, ErrParseError, ErrInstrumentationFailed, ErrExecutionTimeout, ErrExecutionCrash, ErrVersionMismatch, ErrInvalidRequest, ErrCompilationError, ErrInternalError, ErrNotSupported}`
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/property_test.go`
- Change: Replace the hardcoded 4-element `SampledFrom` in `genResponse()` (line 118) with `rapid.SampledFrom(AllErrorCodes)`.

**Step 4.5: Add parity test**
- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-go/protocol/property_test.go`
- Add `TestErrorCodeCountMatchesRegistry` that asserts `len(AllErrorCodes) == 11`.

#### Phase 5: Rust Frontend (shatter-rust)

## Implementation Plan: Error Code Contract Parity (str-fpgb.2)

### Problem Summary

The canonical error code list in `protocol/registry.yaml` defines 11 codes, but every downstream consumer has gaps:

| Location | Count | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| `registry.yaml` (canonical) | 11 | -- |
| `response.schema.json` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `PROTOCOL.md` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| Rust core `ErrorCode` enum | 10 | `not_supported` |
| Rust core `all_error_codes_round_trip` test | 9 | `compilation_error` (enum exists but test omitted) |
| Rust core `arb_error_code()` | 10 | `not_supported` |
| TS `ErrorCode` union type | 10 | `not_supported` |
| TS `arbErrorCode` generator | 10 | `not_supported` |
| Go `constants.go` | 9 | `function_not_found`, `instrumentation_failed` |
| Go `property_test.go` error generator | 4 | 7 codes missing from `SampledFrom` |
| Rust frontend `handler.rs` | uses `"non_executable"` | NOT a standard code |

### Decision: `non_executable` mapping

The `non_executable` error code in `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` (line 383) fires when a function has trait-object parameters that cannot be deserialized. This maps cleanly to the existing `not_supported` code -- "Command not supported by this frontend" covers the case where a function cannot be executed due to language-level constraints. No new code needs to be added to the registry.

### Order of Operations

The guiding principle: fix the canonical source and derived artifacts first (top-down), then fix each consumer, then add parity tests that prevent future drift. Each step is independently testable.

#### Phase 1: Schema and Documentation (no code changes, prevents future drift)

**Step 1.1: Update `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json`**

#### Phase 2: Rust Core (shatter-core)

- File: `/home/ketan/project/shatter/shatter-core/src/protocol.rs` (in the `#[cfg(test)]` module)
- Add a new test `error_code_count_matches_registry` that asserts the number of variants in the `arb_error_code` generator equals the expected count (11). This catches future additions to the enum that aren't reflected in the generator.
- Better approach: add a compile-time exhaustiveness check using a `match` on `ErrorCode` with all variants listed, so that adding a variant without updating the test causes a compiler error:
  ```rust
  #[test]
  fn error_code_enum_is_exhaustive() {
      // This match ensures that if a new ErrorCode variant is added,
      // this test must be updated (and by extension, the round-trip test
      // and arb_error_code generator).
      fn check_exhaustive(code: ErrorCode) -> &'static str {
          match code {
              ErrorCode::FileNotFound => "file_not_found",
              ErrorCode::FunctionNotFound => "function_not_found",
              ErrorCode::ParseError => "parse_error",
              ErrorCode::InstrumentationFailed => "instrumentation_failed",
              ErrorCode::ExecutionTimeout => "execution_timeout",
              ErrorCode::ExecutionCrash => "execution_crash",
              ErrorCode::VersionMismatch => "version_mismatch",
              ErrorCode::InvalidRequest => "invalid_request",
              ErrorCode::CompilationError => "compilation_error",
              ErrorCode::InternalError => "internal_error",
              ErrorCode::NotSupported => "not_supported",
          }
      }
      // Verify the serialized form matches the expected string for each variant.
      let all_codes = [
          ErrorCode::FileNotFound, ErrorCode::FunctionNotFound, ErrorCode::ParseError,
          ErrorCode::InstrumentationFailed, ErrorCode::ExecutionTimeout, ErrorCode::ExecutionCrash,
          ErrorCode::VersionMismatch, ErrorCode::InvalidRequest, ErrorCode::CompilationError,
          ErrorCode::InternalError, ErrorCode::NotSupported,
      ];
      for code in all_codes {
          let expected = check_exhaustive(code.clone());
          let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&code).unwrap();
          assert_eq!(serialized, format!("\"{}\"", expected));
      }
  }
  ```

#### Phase 3: TypeScript Frontend (shatter-ts)

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Validate that protocol/registry.yaml matches the source-of-truth code.
     3→
     4→Checks commands, response statuses, and error codes in:
     5→  - shatter-core/src/protocol.rs  (Rust core)
     6→  - shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts    (TypeScript frontend)
     7→  - shatter-go/protocol/          (Go frontend)
     8→  - shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs  (Rust frontend)
     9→
    10→Exit 0 if everything matches, 1 on any mismatch.
    11→"""
    12→
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import os
    16→import re
    17→import sys
    18→from pathlib import Path
    19→
    20→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→# Resolve paths relative to the repo root (parent of this script's directory)
    22→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    24→REPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent
    25→
    26→REGISTRY_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "protocol" / "registry.yaml"
    27→CORE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-core" / "src" / "protocol.rs"
    28→TS_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-ts" / "src" / "protocol.ts"
    29→GO_CONSTANTS = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "constants.go"
    30→GO_HANDLER = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "handler.go"
    31→RUST_FE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-rust" / "src" / "protocol.rs"
    32→
    33→
    34→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→# Minimal YAML parser — avoids PyYAML dependency
    36→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→
    38→def parse_registry(path: Path) -> dict:
    39→    """Extract commands, statuses, and error_codes from registry.yaml.
    40→
    41→    Returns dict with keys 'commands', 'statuses', 'error_codes' each
    42→    mapping to a set of strings.
    43→    """
    44→    text = path.read_text()
    45→
    46→    def extract_top_level_keys(section: str) -> set[str]:
    47→        """Extract keys under a top-level YAML mapping section."""
    48→        pattern = rf"^{re.escape(section)}:\s*$"
    49→        match = re.search(pattern, text, re.MULTILINE)
    50→        if not match:
    51→            return set()
    52→        start = match.end()
    53→        keys: set[str] = set()
    54→        for line in text[start:].splitlines():
    55→            if not line or line.startswith("#"):
    56→                continue
    57→            # End of section: non-indented, non-blank line
    58→            if line[0] not in (" ", "\t"):
    59→                break
    60→            # Key line: exactly 2-space indent, word followed by colon
    61→            m = re.match(r"^  ([a-z_]+):", line)
    62→            if m:
    63→                keys.add(m.group(1))
    64→        return keys
    65→
    66→    return {
    67→        "commands": extract_top_level_keys("commands"),
    68→        "statuses": extract_top_level_keys("statuses"),
    69→        "error_codes": extract_top_level_keys("error_codes"),
    70→    }
    71→
    72→
    73→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→# Source extractors
    75→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    76→
    77→def extract_rust_core(path: Path) -> dict:
    78→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-core protocol.rs."""
    79→    text = path.read_text()
    80→
    81→    # Commands: enum Command variants (PascalCase → snake_case)
    82→    commands: set[str] = set()
    83→    in_command = False
    84→    for line in text.splitlines():
    85→        if re.match(r"^pub enum Command\b", line):
    86→            in_command = True
    87→            continue
    88→        if in_command:
    89→            if line.startswith("}"):
    90→                break
    91→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
    92→            if m:
    93→                commands.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
    94→
    95→    # ResponseResult: enum variants → statuses
    96→    statuses: set[str] = set()
    97→    in_resp = False
    98→    for line in text.splitlines():
    99→        if re.match(r"^pub enum ResponseResult\b", line):
   100→            in_resp = True
   101→            continue
   102→        if in_resp:
   103→            if line.startswith("}"):
   104→                break
   105→            m = re.match(r"\s+#", line)
   106→            if m:
   107→                continue
   108→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
   109→            if m:
   110→                statuses.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
   111→
   112→    # ErrorCode enum
   113→    error_codes: set[str] = set()
   114→    in_err = False
   115→    for line in text.splitlines():
   116→        if re.match(r"^pub enum ErrorCode\b", line):
   117→            in_err = True
   118→            continue
   119→        if in_err:
   120→            if line.startswith("}"):
   121→                break
   122→            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)", line)
   123→            if m and not m.group(1).startswith("//") and not m.group(1).startswith("#"):
   124→                error_codes.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))
   125→
   126→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   127→
   128→
   129→def extract_ts(path: Path) -> dict:
   130→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-ts protocol.ts."""
   131→    text = path.read_text()
   132→
   133→    commands = extract_ts_union(text, "Command")
   134→    statuses = extract_ts_union(text, "ResponseStatus")
   135→    error_codes = extract_ts_union(text, "ErrorCode")
   136→
   137→    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}
   138→
   139→
   140→def extract_ts_union(text: str, type_name: str) -> set[str]:
   141→    """Extract string literal members from a TypeScript union type."""
   142→    pattern = rf"type\s+{type_name}\s*=\s*([^;]+);"
   143→    m = re.search(pattern, text, re.DOTALL)
   144→    if not m:
   145→        return set()
   146→    return set(re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', m.group(1)))
   147→
   148→
   149→def extract_go(constants_path: Path, handler_path: Path) -> dict:
   150→    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from Go frontend."""
   151→    const_text = constants_path.read_text()
   152→    handler_text = handler_path.read_text()
   153→
   154→    # Error codes from constants.go
   155→    error_codes: set[str] = set()
   156→    for m in re.finditer(r'Err\w+\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"', const_text):
   157→        error_codes.add(m.group(1))
   158→    # Inline error codes in handler.go
   159→    for m in re.finditer(r'Code:\s*"([^"]+)"', handler_text):
   160→        error_codes.add(m.group(1))
   161→

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Validate that protocol/registry.yaml matches the source-of-truth code.

Checks commands, response statuses, and error codes in:
  - shatter-core/src/protocol.rs  (Rust core)
  - shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts    (TypeScript frontend)
  - shatter-go/protocol/          (Go frontend)
  - shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs  (Rust frontend)

Exit 0 if everything matches, 1 on any mismatch.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolve paths relative to the repo root (parent of this script's directory)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
REPO_ROOT = SCRIPT_DIR.parent

REGISTRY_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "protocol" / "registry.yaml"
CORE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-core" / "src" / "protocol.rs"
TS_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-ts" / "src" / "protocol.ts"
GO_CONSTANTS = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "constants.go"
GO_HANDLER = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-go" / "protocol" / "handler.go"
RUST_FE_PROTOCOL = REPO_ROOT / "shatter-rust" / "src" / "protocol.rs"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal YAML parser — avoids PyYAML dependency
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def parse_registry(path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract commands, statuses, and error_codes from registry.yaml.

    Returns dict with keys 'commands', 'statuses', 'error_codes' each
    mapping to a set of strings.
    """
    text = path.read_text()

    def extract_top_level_keys(section: str) -> set[str]:
        """Extract keys under a top-level YAML mapping section."""
        pattern = rf"^{re.escape(section)}:\s*$"
        match = re.search(pattern, text, re.MULTILINE)
        if not match:
            return set()
        start = match.end()
        keys: set[str] = set()
        for line in text[start:].splitlines():
            if not line or line.startswith("#"):
                continue
            # End of section: non-indented, non-blank line
            if line[0] not in (" ", "\t"):
                break
            # Key line: exactly 2-space indent, word followed by colon
            m = re.match(r"^  ([a-z_]+):", line)
            if m:
                keys.add(m.group(1))
        return keys

    return {
        "commands": extract_top_level_keys("commands"),
        "statuses": extract_top_level_keys("statuses"),
        "error_codes": extract_top_level_keys("error_codes"),
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source extractors
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def extract_rust_core(path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-core protocol.rs."""
    text = path.read_text()

    # Commands: enum Command variants (PascalCase → snake_case)
    commands: set[str] = set()
    in_command = False
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if re.match(r"^pub enum Command\b", line):
            in_command = True
            continue
        if in_command:
            if line.startswith("}"):
                break
            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
            if m:
                commands.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))

    # ResponseResult: enum variants → statuses
    statuses: set[str] = set()
    in_resp = False
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if re.match(r"^pub enum ResponseResult\b", line):
            in_resp = True
            continue
        if in_resp:
            if line.startswith("}"):
                break
            m = re.match(r"\s+#", line)
            if m:
                continue
            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)\s*[{(,]", line)
            if m:
                statuses.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))

    # ErrorCode enum
    error_codes: set[str] = set()
    in_err = False
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if re.match(r"^pub enum ErrorCode\b", line):
            in_err = True
            continue
        if in_err:
            if line.startswith("}"):
                break
            m = re.match(r"\s+(\w+)", line)
            if m and not m.group(1).startswith("//") and not m.group(1).startswith("#"):
                error_codes.add(pascal_to_snake(m.group(1)))

    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}


def extract_ts(path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from shatter-ts protocol.ts."""
    text = path.read_text()

    commands = extract_ts_union(text, "Command")
    statuses = extract_ts_union(text, "ResponseStatus")
    error_codes = extract_ts_union(text, "ErrorCode")

    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}


def extract_ts_union(text: str, type_name: str) -> set[str]:
    """Extract string literal members from a TypeScript union type."""
    pattern = rf"type\s+{type_name}\s*=\s*([^;]+);"
    m = re.search(pattern, text, re.DOTALL)
    if not m:
        return set()
    return set(re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', m.group(1)))


def extract_go(constants_path: Path, handler_path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract commands, statuses, error codes from Go frontend."""
    const_text = constants_path.read_text()
    handler_text = handler_path.read_text()

    # Error codes from constants.go
    error_codes: set[str] = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'Err\w+\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"', const_text):
        error_codes.add(m.group(1))
    # Inline error codes in handler.go
    for m in re.finditer(r'Code:\s*"([^"]+)"', handler_text):
        error_codes.add(m.group(1))

    # Commands from CommandCapabilities in constants.go
    commands: set[str] = set()
    cap_match = re.search(r"CommandCapabilities\s*=.*?\{([^}]+)\}", const_text, re.DOTALL)
    if cap_match:
        commands = set(re.findall(r'"([^"]+)"', cap_match.group(1)))
    # Also add handshake and shutdown (always handled, not in capabilities)
    commands.add("handshake")
    commands.add("shutdown")

    # Statuses from handler.go: resp.Status = "..."
    statuses: set[str] = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'\.Status\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"', handler_text):
        statuses.add(m.group(1))

    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}


def extract_rust_fe(path: Path) -> dict:
    """Extract commands and error codes from shatter-rust protocol.rs."""
    text = path.read_text()

    # Commands: look for command string matches in dispatch
    commands: set[str] = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'"(handshake|analyze|instrument|execute|setup|teardown|generate|shutdown)"', text):
        commands.add(m.group(1))

    # Error codes
    error_codes: set[str] = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'code.*?"([a-z_]+)"', text):
        val = m.group(1)
        # Filter to actual error codes (not random strings)
        if val in {
            "file_not_found", "function_not_found", "parse_error",
            "instrumentation_failed", "execution_timeout", "execution_crash",
            "version_mismatch", "invalid_request", "compilation_error",
            "internal_error", "not_supported",
        }:
            error_codes.add(val)

    # Statuses
    statuses: set[str] = set()
    for m in re.finditer(r'status.*?"([a-z_]+)"', text):
        statuses.add(m.group(1))

    return {"commands": commands, "statuses": statuses, "error_codes": error_codes}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def pascal_to_snake(name: str) -> str:
    """Convert PascalCase to snake_case."""
    s1 = re.sub(r"(.)([A-Z][a-z]+)", r"\1_\2", name)
    return re.sub(r"([a-z0-9])([A-Z])", r"\1_\2", s1).lower()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def validate(registry: dict, source_name: str, source: dict) -> list[str]:
    """Compare registry entries against a source; return list of issues."""
    issues: list[str] = []
    for category in ("commands", "statuses", "error_codes"):
        reg_set = registry[category]
        src_set = source.get(category, set())
        if not src_set:
            continue

        missing_from_registry = src_set - reg_set
        missing_from_source = reg_set - src_set

        for item in sorted(missing_from_registry):
            issues.append(
                f"  {category}: '{item}' found in {source_name} but missing from registry"
            )
        # Only warn about missing-from-source for commands (frontends may not
        # implement every command/status/error code)
        if category == "commands":
            for item in sorted(missing_from_source):
                issues.append(
                    f"  {category}: '{item}' in registry but not found in {source_name} (may be unimplemented)"
                )
    return issues


def main() -> int:
    if not REGISTRY_PATH.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: Registry not found at {REGISTRY_PATH}")
        return 1

    registry = parse_registry(REGISTRY_PATH)
    print(f"Registry: {len(registry['commands'])} commands, "
          f"{len(registry['statuses'])} statuses, "
          f"{len(registry['error_codes'])} error codes")

    all_issues: list[str] = []

    # --- Rust core (authoritative) ---
    if CORE_PROTOCOL.exists():
        core = extract_rust_core(CORE_PROTOCOL)
        issues = validate(registry, "shatter-core", core)
        if issues:
            all_issues.append("shatter-core/src/protocol.rs:")
            all_issues.extend(issues)
    else:
        all_issues.append(f"WARNING: {CORE_PROTOCOL} not found")

    # --- TypeScript frontend ---
    if TS_PROTOCOL.exists():
        ts = extract_ts(TS_PROTOCOL)
        issues = validate(registry, "shatter-ts", ts)
        if issues:
            all_issues.append("shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts:")
            all_issues.extend(issues)
    else:
        all_issues.append(f"WARNING: {TS_PROTOCOL} not found")

    # --- Go frontend ---
    if GO_CONSTANTS.exists() and GO_HANDLER.exists():
        go = extract_go(GO_CONSTANTS, GO_HANDLER)
        issues = validate(registry, "shatter-go", go)
        if issues:
            all_issues.append("shatter-go/protocol/:")
            all_issues.extend(issues)
    else:
        all_issues.append(f"WARNING: Go protocol files not found")

    # --- Rust frontend ---
    if RUST_FE_PROTOCOL.exists():
        rust_fe = extract_rust_fe(RUST_FE_PROTOCOL)
        issues = validate(registry, "shatter-rust", rust_fe)
        if issues:
            all_issues.append("shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs:")
            all_issues.extend(issues)
    else:
        all_issues.append(f"WARNING: {RUST_FE_PROTOCOL} not found")

    # --- Report ---
    if all_issues:
        # Separate hard errors from informational warnings
        errors = [i for i in all_issues if "missing from registry" in i]
        warnings = [i for i in all_issues if "missing from registry" not in i]

        if warnings:
            print("\nWarnings:")
            for w in warnings:
                print(f"  {w}")

        if errors:
            print("\nERRORS (items in source but missing from registry):")
            for e in errors:
                print(f"  {e}")
            return 1

        print("\nAll checks passed (with informational warnings).")
        return 0
    else:
        print("All checks passed.")
        return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

input.content:
# str-fpgb.2: Error Code Contract Parity

## Context

Error code lists have drifted across the protocol registry, JSON schema, docs, and all three frontends. The canonical source (`protocol/registry.yaml`) defines 11 error codes, but every downstream consumer is missing at least one. The existing `validate-protocol-registry.py` script doesn't catch this because it only flags codes present in source but absent from registry — not the reverse for error codes.

### Current Drift

| Location | Count | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| `protocol/registry.yaml` (canonical) | 11 | — |
| `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `PROTOCOL.md` error codes table | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `shatter-core` `ErrorCode` enum | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-ts` `ErrorCode` type | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-ts` `arbErrorCode` generator | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-go` constants | 9 | `ErrFunctionNotFound`, `ErrInstrumentationFailed` (used as string literals) |
| `shatter-rust` handler | uses `"non_executable"` | not a standard code |

## Plan

### Phase 1: Failing tests first (bugfix workflow)

**1a. Rust core — exhaustive parity test** (`shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`, test module)
- Add `error_code_parity_with_registry` test that lists all 11 expected codes and asserts each deserializes to an `ErrorCode` variant. This fails immediately because `NotSupported` doesn't exist yet.

**1b. Go — parity test** (`shatter-go/protocol/parity_test.go`)
- Add `TestAllErrorCodesDefinedAsConstants` that checks all 11 expected codes exist as constants. Fails because `ErrFunctionNotFound` and `ErrInstrumentationFailed` are missing.

**1c. TS — parity test** (`shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`)
- Add test that asserts the `ErrorCode` type covers all 11 codes. Fails because `not_supported` is missing.

### Phase 2: Fix schema and docs

**2a. `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json`** (line 184-188)
- Add `"compilation_error"` and `"not_supported"` to the enum array.

**2b. `PROTOCOL.md`** (line 322-332)
- Add rows for `compilation_error` and `not_supported` to the Error Codes table.

### Phase 3: Fix Rust core

**3a. `shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`** (~line 458)
- Add `NotSupported` variant to `ErrorCode` enum with doc comment.

**3b. `shatter-core/src/test_arbitraries.rs`**
- Add `Just(ErrorCode::NotSupported)` to `arb_error_code()`.

### Phase 4: Fix TypeScript

**4a. `shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts`** (line 135-145)
- Add `ALL_ERROR_CODES` const array with all 11 codes.
- Derive `ErrorCode` type from it: `type ErrorCode = (typeof ALL_ERROR_CODES)[number]`.

**4b. `shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`** (line 83-87)
- Update `arbErrorCode` to use `ALL_ERROR_CODES`.

### Phase 5: Fix Go

**5a. `shatter-go/protocol/constants.go`**
- Add `ErrFunctionNotFound` and `ErrInstrumentationFailed` constants.
- Add `AllErrorCodes` slice referencing all 11 constants.

**5b. `shatter-go/protocol/handler.go`** (lines 187, 281, 283)
- Replace string literals with constants.

**5c. `shatter-go/protocol/handler_test.go`** (lines 258, 439)
- Replace string literals with constants.

**5d. `shatter-go/protocol/property_test.go`**
- Update error code generator to use `AllErrorCodes`.

### Phase 6: Fix Rust frontend

**6a. `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs`** (line 383)
- Replace `"non_executable"` with `"not_supported"`.

**6b. `shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs`** or new `constants.rs`
- Add `ERR_*` string constants for all 11 error codes.
- Replace string literals in `handler.rs` with constants.

**6c. Parity test** in `shatter-rust`
- Test that all 11 codes are defined and round-trip correctly.

### Phase 7: Strengthen validator

**7a. `scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py`**
- Make error code missing-from-source a hard error (not just commands).
- Remove hardcoded allowlist in `extract_rust_fe` — extract dynamically from `ERR_*` constants.
- Add JSON schema enum validation against registry.

### Verification

1. `cargo test -p shatter-core` — parity test passes
2. `cd shatter-ts && npm test` — parity test passes
3. `cd shatter-go && go test ./...` — parity test passes
4. `cargo test -p shatter-rust` — parity test passes
5. `python3 scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py` — zero warnings/errors
6. `cargo clippy -p shatter-core -p shatter-rust -- -D warnings` — clean

### Critical Files

- `protocol/registry.yaml` — canonical source (read-only, already correct)

# str-fpgb.2: Error Code Contract Parity

## Context

Error code lists have drifted across the protocol registry, JSON schema, docs, and all three frontends. The canonical source (`protocol/registry.yaml`) defines 11 error codes, but every downstream consumer is missing at least one. The existing `validate-protocol-registry.py` script doesn't catch this because it only flags codes present in source but absent from registry — not the reverse for error codes.

### Current Drift

| Location | Count | Missing |
|---|---|---|
| `protocol/registry.yaml` (canonical) | 11 | — |
| `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json` | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `PROTOCOL.md` error codes table | 9 | `compilation_error`, `not_supported` |
| `shatter-core` `ErrorCode` enum | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-ts` `ErrorCode` type | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-ts` `arbErrorCode` generator | 10 | `not_supported` |
| `shatter-go` constants | 9 | `ErrFunctionNotFound`, `ErrInstrumentationFailed` (used as string literals) |
| `shatter-rust` handler | uses `"non_executable"` | not a standard code |

## Plan

### Phase 1: Failing tests first (bugfix workflow)

**1a. Rust core — exhaustive parity test** (`shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`, test module)
- Add `error_code_parity_with_registry` test that lists all 11 expected codes and asserts each deserializes to an `ErrorCode` variant. This fails immediately because `NotSupported` doesn't exist yet.

**1b. Go — parity test** (`shatter-go/protocol/parity_test.go`)
- Add `TestAllErrorCodesDefinedAsConstants` that checks all 11 expected codes exist as constants. Fails because `ErrFunctionNotFound` and `ErrInstrumentationFailed` are missing.

**1c. TS — parity test** (`shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`)
- Add test that asserts the `ErrorCode` type covers all 11 codes. Fails because `not_supported` is missing.

### Phase 2: Fix schema and docs

**2a. `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json`** (line 184-188)
- Add `"compilation_error"` and `"not_supported"` to the enum array.

**2b. `PROTOCOL.md`** (line 322-332)
- Add rows for `compilation_error` and `not_supported` to the Error Codes table.

### Phase 3: Fix Rust core

**3a. `shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`** (~line 458)
- Add `NotSupported` variant to `ErrorCode` enum with doc comment.

**3b. `shatter-core/src/test_arbitraries.rs`**
- Add `Just(ErrorCode::NotSupported)` to `arb_error_code()`.

### Phase 4: Fix TypeScript

**4a. `shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts`** (line 135-145)
- Add `ALL_ERROR_CODES` const array with all 11 codes.
- Derive `ErrorCode` type from it: `type ErrorCode = (typeof ALL_ERROR_CODES)[number]`.

**4b. `shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts`** (line 83-87)
- Update `arbErrorCode` to use `ALL_ERROR_CODES`.

### Phase 5: Fix Go

**5a. `shatter-go/protocol/constants.go`**
- Add `ErrFunctionNotFound` and `ErrInstrumentationFailed` constants.
- Add `AllErrorCodes` slice referencing all 11 constants.

**5b. `shatter-go/protocol/handler.go`** (lines 187, 281, 283)
- Replace string literals with constants.

**5c. `shatter-go/protocol/handler_test.go`** (lines 258, 439)
- Replace string literals with constants.

**5d. `shatter-go/protocol/property_test.go`**
- Update error code generator to use `AllErrorCodes`.

### Phase 6: Fix Rust frontend

**6a. `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs`** (line 383)
- Replace `"non_executable"` with `"not_supported"`.

**6b. `shatter-rust/src/protocol.rs`** or new `constants.rs`
- Add `ERR_*` string constants for all 11 error codes.
- Replace string literals in `handler.rs` with constants.

**6c. Parity test** in `shatter-rust`
- Test that all 11 codes are defined and round-trip correctly.

### Phase 7: Strengthen validator

**7a. `scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py`**
- Make error code missing-from-source a hard error (not just commands).
- Remove hardcoded allowlist in `extract_rust_fe` — extract dynamically from `ERR_*` constants.
- Add JSON schema enum validation against registry.

### Verification

1. `cargo test -p shatter-core` — parity test passes
2. `cd shatter-ts && npm test` — parity test passes
3. `cd shatter-go && go test ./...` — parity test passes
4. `cargo test -p shatter-rust` — parity test passes
5. `python3 scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py` — zero warnings/errors
6. `cargo clippy -p shatter-core -p shatter-rust -- -D warnings` — clean

### Critical Files

- `protocol/registry.yaml` — canonical source (read-only, already correct)
- `protocol/schemas/response.schema.json` — add 2 missing codes
- `PROTOCOL.md` — add 2 missing rows
- `shatter-core/src/protocol.rs` — add `NotSupported` variant + parity test
- `shatter-core/src/test_arbitraries.rs` — update generator
- `shatter-ts/src/protocol.ts` — `ALL_ERROR_CODES` + derive type
- `shatter-ts/src/property.test.ts` — update generator + parity test
- `shatter-go/protocol/constants.go` — add 2 constants + `AllErrorCodes`
- `shatter-go/protocol/handler.go` — replace literals
- `shatter-go/protocol/handler_test.go` — replace literals
- `shatter-rust/src/handler.rs` — fix `non_executable` → `not_supported`
- `scripts/validate-protocol-registry.py` — stricter validation

pub fn arb_error_code() -> impl Strategy<Value = ErrorCode> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(ErrorCode::FileNotFound),
        Just(ErrorCode::FunctionNotFound),
        Just(ErrorCode::ParseError),
        Just(ErrorCode::InstrumentationFailed),
        Just(ErrorCode::ExecutionTimeout),
        Just(ErrorCode::ExecutionCrash),
        Just(ErrorCode::VersionMismatch),
        Just(ErrorCode::InvalidRequest),
        Just(ErrorCode::CompilationError),
        Just(ErrorCode::InternalError),
    ]
}

     2→//!
     3→//! These types match the JSON wire format defined in `shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`.
     4→//! The protocol uses newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) over stdin/stdout between
     5→//! the core engine and this frontend.
     6→//!
     7→//! Like the Go frontend, we use flat structs with optional fields rather than
     8→//! tagged enums — simpler for a standalone frontend that only needs to parse
     9→//! requests and emit responses.
    10→
    11→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    12→use std::collections::HashMap;
    13→
    14→/// Well-known complex types beyond primitives and structural types.
    15→/// Matches `ComplexKind` in shatter-core/src/types.rs.
    16→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    17→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    18→pub enum ComplexKind {
    19→    Date, DateTime, Time, Duration,
    20→    RegExp, Char, Symbol,
    21→    BigInt, BigDecimal, Complex, Rational, Range,
    22→    Buffer, BitSet,
    23→    Error, Option, Result,
    24→    Closure, Iterator,
    25→    Url, IpAddress,
    26→    Uuid,
    27→    Path,
    28→    Money, SemVer, Email, MimeType, Color, GeoPoint, Locale,
    29→    Rune, GoByte,
    30→}
    31→
    32→/// Describes the type of a value, as reported by a language frontend.
    33→/// Matches `TypeInfo` in shatter-core/src/types.rs.
    34→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    35→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    36→pub enum TypeInfo {
    37→    Int,
    38→    Float,
    39→    Str,
    40→    Bool,
    41→    Array { element: Box<TypeInfo> },
    42→    Object { fields: Vec<(String, TypeInfo)> },
    43→    Union { variants: Vec<TypeInfo> },
    44→    Nullable { inner: Box<TypeInfo> },
    45→    Complex {
    46→        #[serde(rename = "complex_kind")]
    47→        kind: ComplexKind,
    48→        #[serde(default)]
    49→        metadata: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
    50→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    51→        inner: Option<Box<TypeInfo>>,
    52→    },
    53→    Opaque {
    54→        label: String,
    55→    },
    56→    Unknown,
    57→}
    58→
    59→/// Binary operation kind for symbolic expressions.
    60→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    61→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    62→pub enum BinOpKind {
    63→    Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge,
    64→    Add, Sub, Mul, Div, Mod,
    65→    And, Or,
    66→    BitwiseAnd, BitwiseOr, BitwiseXor,
    67→    In, InstanceOf,
    68→}
    69→
    70→/// Unary operation kind for symbolic expressions.
    71→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    72→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    73→pub enum UnOpKind {
    74→    Not,
    75→    Neg,
    76→    BitwiseNot,
    77→    #[serde(rename = "typeof")]
    78→    TypeOf,
    79→}
    80→
    81→/// Constant value in a symbolic expression.
    82→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    83→#[serde(tag = "type", content = "value", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    84→pub enum ConstValue {
    85→    Int(i64),
    86→    Float(f64),
    87→    Str(String),
    88→    Bool(bool),
    89→    Null,
    90→}
    91→
    92→/// Symbolic expression tree for branch conditions.
    93→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    94→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    95→pub enum SymExpr {
    96→    Param {
    97→        name: String,
    98→        #[serde(default)]
    99→        path: Vec<String>,
   100→    },
   101→    Const(ConstValue),
   102→    BinOp {
   103→        op: BinOpKind,
   104→        left: Box<SymExpr>,
   105→        right: Box<SymExpr>,
   106→    },
   107→    UnOp {
   108→        op: UnOpKind,
   109→        operand: Box<SymExpr>,
   110→    },
   111→    Call {
   112→        name: String,
   113→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   114→        receiver: Option<Box<SymExpr>>,
   115→        #[serde(default)]
   116→        args: Vec<SymExpr>,
   117→    },
   118→    Unknown,
   119→}
   120→
   121→/// Branch type in control flow.
   122→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   123→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   124→pub enum BranchType {
   125→    If,
   126→    ElseIf,
   127→    Switch,
   128→    Ternary,
   129→    LogicalAnd,
   130→    LogicalOr,
   131→    While,
   132→    For,
   133→}
   134→
   135→/// A branch point in a function's control flow.
   136→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   137→pub struct BranchInfo {
   138→    pub id: u32,
   139→    pub line: u32,
   140→    pub condition_text: String,
   141→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   142→    pub condition: Option<SymExpr>,
   143→    pub branch_type: BranchType,
   144→}
   145→
   146→/// Parameter info for a function.
   147→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   148→pub struct ParamInfo {
   149→    pub name: String,
   150→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
   151→    pub typ: TypeInfo,
   152→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   153→    pub type_name: Option<String>,
   154→}
   155→
   156→/// Kind of external dependency.
   157→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   158→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   159→pub enum DependencyKind {
   160→    FunctionCall,
   161→    MethodCall,
   162→    PropertyAccess,
   163→    ModuleImport,
   164→}
   165→
   166→/// An external dependency detected in a function body.
   167→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   168→pub struct ExternalDependency {
   169→    pub kind: DependencyKind,
   170→    pub symbol: String,
   171→    pub source_module: String,
   172→    pub return_type: TypeInfo,
   173→    pub param_types: Vec<TypeInfo>,
   174→    pub call_sites: Vec<u32>,
   175→}
   176→
   177→/// A literal constant extracted from source code for use as a candidate test input.
   178→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   179→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   180→pub enum LiteralValue {
   181→    Int { value: i64 },
   182→    Float { value: f64 },
   183→    Str { value: String },
   184→    Bool { value: bool },
   185→    Regex { pattern: String },
   186→}
   187→
   188→/// A detected cryptographic API boundary within a function.
   189→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   190→pub struct CryptoBoundary {
   191→    pub symbol: String,
   192→    pub source_module: String,
   193→    pub direction: String,
   194→    pub output: String,
   195→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "HashMap::is_empty")]
   196→    pub param_roles: HashMap<String, String>,
   197→    pub call_sites: Vec<u32>,
   198→}
   199→
   200→/// Analysis result for a single function.
   201→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   202→pub struct FunctionAnalysis {
   203→    pub name: String,
   204→    #[serde(default)]
   205→    pub exported: bool,
   206→    pub params: Vec<ParamInfo>,
   207→    pub branches: Vec<BranchInfo>,
   208→    pub dependencies: Vec<ExternalDependency>,
   209→    pub return_type: TypeInfo,
   210→    pub start_line: u32,
   211→    pub end_line: u32,
   212→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   213→    pub literals: Vec<LiteralValue>,
   214→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   215→    pub crypto_boundaries: Vec<CryptoBoundary>,
   216→}
   217→
   218→/// Current protocol version.
   219→pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: &str = "0.1.0";
   220→
   221→/// Frontend version.
   222→pub const FRONTEND_VERSION: &str = "0.1.0";
   223→
   224→/// Language identifier for this frontend.
   225→pub const FRONTEND_LANGUAGE: &str = "rust";
   226→
   227→/// Granularity level for setup/teardown lifecycle management.
   228→/// Matches `SetupLevel` in shatter-core/src/protocol.rs.
   229→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   230→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   231→pub enum SetupLevel {
   232→    Session,
   233→    File,
   234→    Function,
   235→    Execution,
   236→}
   237→
   238→/// A single entry in a setup context stack, associating a lifecycle level
   239→/// with the opaque context value returned by its Setup command.
   240→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   241→pub struct SetupContextEntry {
   242→    pub level: SetupLevel,
   243→    pub context: serde_json::Value,
   244→}
   245→
   246→/// Stack of active setup contexts, ordered from outermost (session) to
   247→/// innermost (execution). Passed to Execute so frontends can restore
   248→/// all active setup state.
   249→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   250→pub struct SetupContextStack {
   251→    pub contexts: Vec<SetupContextEntry>,
   252→}
   253→
   254→/// A request message from the core engine to this frontend.
   255→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
   256→pub struct Request {
   257→    pub protocol_version: String,
   258→    pub id: u64,
   259→    pub command: String,
   260→
   261→    // Handshake fields
   262→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when capability negotiation is implemented
   263→    #[serde(default)]
   264→    pub capabilities: Vec<String>,
   265→
   266→    // Analyze/Instrument/Execute fields
   267→    #[serde(default)]
   268→    pub file: Option<String>,
   269→    #[serde(default)]
   270→    pub function: Option<String>,
   271→
   272→    // Execute fields
   273→    #[serde(default)]
   274→    pub inputs: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   275→    #[serde(default)]
   276→    pub mocks: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   277→    /// Stack of active setup contexts from enclosing Setup commands, if any.
   278→    #[allow(dead_code)] // carried on Execute requests; handler will forward when execute passes context
   279→    #[serde(default)]
   280→    pub setup_context: Option<SetupContextStack>,
   281→
   282→    // Setup fields
   283→    /// Lifecycle level for this setup/teardown (session, file, function, execution).
   284→    #[serde(default)]
   285→    pub level: Option<SetupLevel>,
   286→    /// Scope identifier (function name, file path, or session label).
   287→    #[serde(default)]
   288→    pub scope: Option<String>,
   289→    /// Parent context stack from enclosing setup levels.
   290→    #[serde(default)]
   291→    pub parent_context: Option<SetupContextStack>,
   292→
   293→    // Generate fields
   294→    /// Name of the type or parameter to generate a value for.
   295→    #[serde(default)]
   296→    pub name: Option<String>,
   297→    /// Whether the generator targets a type name or a parameter name.
   298→    #[allow(dead_code)] // parsed from protocol but not used in dispatch logic yet
   299→    #[serde(default)]
   300→    pub kind: Option<String>,
   301→    /// Opaque recipe state from a prior generate call, enabling stateful generators.
   302→    #[serde(default)]
   303→    pub recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   304→
   305→    // Project context
   306→    /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   307→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when full analysis is implemented
   308→    #[serde(default)]
   309→    pub project_root: Option<String>,
   310→}
   311→
   312→/// A response message from this frontend to the core engine.
   313→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   314→pub struct Response {
   315→    pub protocol_version: String,
   316→    pub id: u64,
   317→    pub status: String,
   318→
   319→    // Handshake fields
   320→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   321→    pub frontend_version: Option<String>,
   322→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   323→    pub language: Option<String>,
   324→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   325→    pub capabilities: Option<Vec<String>>,
   326→
   327→    // Setup fields
   328→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   329→    pub setup_context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   330→
   331→    // Generate fields
   332→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   333→    pub value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   334→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   335→    pub generator_id: Option<String>,
   336→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   337→    pub recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   338→
   339→    // Instrument fields
   340→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   341→    pub instrumented: Option<bool>,
   342→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   343→    pub output_file: Option<String>,
   344→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   345→    pub instrumentable_line_count: Option<u32>,
   346→
   347→    // Analyze fields
   348→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   349→    pub functions: Option<Vec<FunctionAnalysis>>,
   350→
   351→    // Execute result fields — flattened to match the core's wire format.
   352→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   353→    pub return_value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   354→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   355→    pub thrown_error: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   356→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   357→    pub branch_path: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   358→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   359→    pub lines_executed: Option<Vec<u32>>,
   360→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   361→    pub calls_to_external: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   362→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   363→    pub path_constraints: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   364→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   365→    pub side_effects: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   366→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   367→    pub performance: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   368→
   369→    // Error fields
   370→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   371→    pub code: Option<String>,
   372→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   373→    pub message: Option<String>,
   374→}
   375→
   376→impl Response {
   377→    /// Create a base response with protocol version and request ID.
   378→    pub fn base(id: u64) -> Self {
   379→        Self {
   380→            protocol_version: PROTOCOL_VERSION.to_string(),
   381→            id,
   382→            status: String::new(),
   383→            frontend_version: None,
   384→            language: None,
   385→            capabilities: None,
   386→            setup_context: None,
   387→            value: None,
   388→            generator_id: None,
   389→            recipe: None,
   390→            instrumented: None,
   391→            output_file: None,
   392→            instrumentable_line_count: None,
   393→            functions: None,
   394→            return_value: None,
   395→            thrown_error: None,
   396→            branch_path: None,
   397→            lines_executed: None,
   398→            calls_to_external: None,
   399→            path_constraints: None,
   400→            side_effects: None,
   401→            performance: None,
   402→            code: None,
   403→            message: None,
   404→        }
   405→    }
   406→}

   408→#[cfg(test)]
   409→mod tests {
   410→    use super::*;
   411→
   412→    fn round_trip<T: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + PartialEq + std::fmt::Debug>(
   413→        value: &T,
   414→    ) {
   415→        let json = serde_json::to_string(value).expect("serialize");
   416→        let deserialized: T = serde_json::from_str(&json).expect("deserialize");
   417→        assert_eq!(*value, deserialized, "round-trip failed for json: {json}");
   418→    }
   419→
   420→    #[test]
   421→    fn typeinfo_opaque_round_trips() {
   422→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Opaque {
   423→            label: "net.Socket".to_string(),
   424→        });
   425→    }
   426→
   427→    #[test]
   428→    fn typeinfo_opaque_serializes_with_correct_kind() {
   429→        let ti = TypeInfo::Opaque {
   430→            label: "fs.FileHandle".to_string(),
   431→        };
   432→        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&ti).expect("serialize");
   433→        assert_eq!(json["kind"], "opaque");
   434→        assert_eq!(json["label"], "fs.FileHandle");
   435→    }
   436→
   437→    #[test]
   438→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_array_round_trips() {
   439→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Array {
   440→            element: Box::new(TypeInfo::Opaque {
   441→                label: "stream.Readable".to_string(),
   442→            }),
   443→        });
   444→    }
   445→
   446→    #[test]
   447→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_nullable_round_trips() {
   448→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Nullable {
   449→            inner: Box::new(TypeInfo::Opaque {
   450→                label: "channel".to_string(),
   451→            }),
   452→        });
   453→    }
   454→
   455→    #[test]
   456→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_object_round_trips() {
   457→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Object {
   458→            fields: vec![
   459→                (
   460→                    "conn".into(),
   461→                    TypeInfo::Opaque {
   462→                        label: "pg.Client".to_string(),
   463→                    },
   464→                ),
   465→                ("name".into(), TypeInfo::Str),
   466→            ],
   467→        });
   468→    }
   469→
   470→    #[test]
   471→    fn existing_typeinfo_variants_still_round_trip() {
   472→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Int);
   473→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Float);
   474→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Str);
   475→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Bool);
   476→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Unknown);
   477→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Array {
   478→            element: Box::new(TypeInfo::Int),
   479→        });
   480→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Nullable {
   481→            inner: Box::new(TypeInfo::Str),
   482→        });
   483→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Object {
   484→            fields: vec![("x".into(), TypeInfo::Int)],
   485→        });
   486→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Union {
   487→            variants: vec![TypeInfo::Str, TypeInfo::Int],
   488→        });
   489→    }
   490→
   491→    #[test]
   492→    fn typeinfo_complex_still_round_trips() {
   493→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Complex {
   494→            kind: ComplexKind::Date,
   495→            metadata: HashMap::new(),
   496→            inner: None,

   499→
   500→    #[test]
   501→    fn opaque_in_function_analysis_json_deserializes() {
   502→        // Verify TypeInfo::Opaque works when embedded in a FunctionAnalysis-shaped JSON,
   503→        // parsed as a generic Value and then extracting the type field.
   504→        let json = r#"{"kind": "opaque", "label": "stream.Readable"}"#;
   505→        let param_type: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize param type");
   506→        assert_eq!(
   507→            param_type,
   508→            TypeInfo::Opaque {
   509→                label: "stream.Readable".to_string(),
   510→            }
   511→        );
   512→
   513→        // Nested inside an object field (simulating a return_type in analysis results)
   514→        let nested_json = r#"{"kind": "object", "fields": [["conn", {"kind": "opaque", "label": "pg.Client"}], ["ready", {"kind": "bool"}]]}"#;
   515→        let nested: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(nested_json).expect("deserialize nested");
   516→        if let TypeInfo::Object { fields } = &nested {
   517→            assert_eq!(fields.len(), 2);
   518→            assert_eq!(
   519→                fields[0].1,
   520→                TypeInfo::Opaque {
   521→                    label: "pg.Client".to_string(),
   522→                }
   523→            );
   524→        } else {
   525→            panic!("expected Object, got {:?}", nested);
   526→        }
   527→    }
